Operation 'reverseGeocode' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Current description is somewhat redundant and lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. Improved version clarifies: (1) exact input parameters expected (lat/lon, radius), (2) specific output types (addresses, POIs, stops) consistent with API context, (3) practical use cases (GPS, map clicks) for AI agent decision-making, (4) that results are ranked, and (5) that feature properties and geometries are returned. This helps AI agents understand when reverse geocoding is appropriate and what to expect.

data.current_description: Find features within a geographical area defined by a coordinate and radius. Converts geographic coordinates to a description of a location.
data.description_suggestion: Reverse geocode coordinates to find nearby features. Accepts a geographic coordinate (latitude/longitude) and optional search radius to identify addresses, POIs, and public transport stops near the specified location. Returns a ranked list of matching features with their properties and geometries. Useful for determining location context from GPS coordinates or map clicks.
data.reasoning: Current description is somewhat redundant and lacks specificity about inputs and outputs. Improved version clarifies: (1) exact input parameters expected (lat/lon, radius), (2) specific output types (addresses, POIs, stops) consistent with API context, (3) practical use cases (GPS, map clicks) for AI agent decision-making, (4) that results are ranked, and (5) that feature properties and geometries are returned. This helps AI agents understand when reverse geocoding is appropriate and what to expect.
